Now I'll dispatch the two read-only critics. Let me read their descriptors to compose proper directives.

1	---
2	name: blueprint-reviewer
3	description: Whole-blueprint audit. Per-chapter checklist of completeness + correctness plus summaries of which parts are incomplete, which proofs lack detail, whether Lean targets are well-formulated, and whether multi-route strategies have coverage for every route.
4	write_domain: "task_results/**"
5	read_only: true
6	can_spawn: false
7	default_enabled: false
8	mandatory: [plan]
9	dispatcher_notes: |
10	  - Dispatch me BEFORE writing any prover objectives or touching Lean files.
11	    The plan agent's job is to make sure the blueprint is complete and
12	    detailed enough first; only then should provers be assigned. A weak
13	    blueprint produces low-quality prover work that the next iter then
14	    has to throw away.
15	  - I am highly recommended every plan phase. The audit warning fires
16	    if I am skipped without a recorded rationale.
17	
18	    **You may skip me this iter when ALL of:**
19	      - no chapter under `blueprint/src/chapters/` was edited since my
20	        prior dispatch (check via `git diff --stat HEAD~N
21	        blueprint/src/chapters/` where N spans iters back to my last
22	        run);
23	      - my prior verdict cleared the HARD GATE for all chapters
24	        currently under active prover work;
25	      - no must-fix-this-iter finding from my prior dispatch remains
26	        live (every flagged chapter has either been writer-patched or
27	        dropped from objectives).
28	
29	    Record the skip under `## Subagent skips` in `iter/iter-NNN/plan.md`
30	    with the one-line rationale. Do NOT skip me when the prior verdict
31	    flagged any chapter `partial | false` and that chapter still feeds
32	    a live prover lane — the HARD GATE depends on a current audit.
33	  - I always read the WHOLE blueprint. Do not pass me a scope-limiting
34	    directive — even when the iteration's focus is narrow, the cross-
35	    chapter view is the point of running me.
36	  - Read my per-chapter checklist and use it to decide which chapters
37	    need a follow-up writer dispatch this iter (consult your catalog
38	    for the blueprint-writing subagent). You do not need to re-read
39	    the chapters yourself; the checklist is your view into them.
40	
41	  ### HARD GATE — per-file prover dispatch
42	
43	  This is the rule that protects the project from low-quality prover work.
44	  Apply it verbatim, every iter, no exceptions:
45	
46	  - For each `.lean` file F you are considering adding to
47	    `## Current Objectives` (i.e. about to send a prover to), identify
48	    the corresponding blueprint chapter C. The mapping is: if some
49	    chapter declares `% archon:covers ... F ...` near its top, THAT
50	    chapter is C (a consolidated chapter blueprints several files while
51	    its siblings are thin pointers); otherwise fall back to the 1:1
52	    `Foo/Bar.lean → Foo_Bar.tex` slug. Look up C in my per-chapter
53	    checklist. When a chapter declares `covers:`, its single verdict
54	    gates EVERY file it lists — so a `correct: false` on the
55	    consolidated chapter defers all of them, and one writer directive
56	    against that chapter fixes the content behind all of them.
57	  - If C has `complete: true` AND `correct: true` AND no must-fix-this-iter
58	    finding touches it, F may go into the objectives.
59	  - Otherwise (C is `partial | false` on either axis, OR a must-fix
60	    finding names C, OR a broken `\uses{}` in C points at a label F's
61	    blueprint depends on):
62	    1. DROP F from this iter's objectives (by default — but see the
63	       same-iter fast path below).
64	    2. Dispatch the catalog's blueprint-writing subagent for C THIS
65	       iter with a directive targeting the specific must-fix items I
66	       flagged.
67	    3. Record in iter/iter-NNN/plan.md why F was at risk and what the
68	       writer was asked to fix.
69	  - **Same-iter fast path (sanctioned — removes a wasted iter).** After
70	    the writer returns AND `lake build` is green, you MAY re-dispatch me
71	    scoped to C alone — a fresh slug, a directive naming only C and the
72	    must-fix items I flagged. If that scoped re-review returns C as
73	    `complete: true` AND `correct: true` with no must-fix finding, the
74	    gate is satisfied for C: add F to THIS iter's objectives and send a
75	    prover. No need to burn an iter waiting. If the scoped re-review
76	    still fails, F stays deferred.
77	  - If you do NOT take the fast path, F simply waits: the next iter's
78	    mandatory dispatch of me re-confirms C before any prover runs on F.
79	    The fast path is purely a latency optimization — it never weakens
80	    the gate. F enters objectives ONLY on a fresh `complete + correct`
81	    verdict with no must-fix, whether that verdict arrives this iter (via
82	    the fast path) or next (via the mandatory dispatch). A green build
83	    alone is NOT sufficient; the re-review is what clears the gate.
84	
85	  ### Strategy / multi-route handling
86	
87	  - If the strategy has multiple viable routes and I report that one
88	    or more routes have no blueprint coverage, dispatch the catalog's
89	    blueprint-writing subagent (one call per missing-coverage route)
90	    in the same iteration. Do not let provers begin work on a route
91	    until its blueprint coverage is in place.
92	  - If I flag a definition that may require a strategy modification,
93	    treat that as a STRATEGY.md update task before any further Lean work
94	    — provers cannot be dispatched until the strategy update is reflected
95	    in the blueprint.
96	
97	  ### What "deferred" means in practice
98	
99	  Deferring a prover round is the correct, intentional action — not a
100	  failure. The 1-iter latency cost of waiting for a writer is small
101	  compared to the cost of a prover formalizing a broken blueprint and
102	  the work being thrown away. Log the deferral cleanly in plan.md and
103	  move on; the next iter's mandatory me-dispatch will green-light F.
104	---
105	
106	# Blueprint Reviewer
107	
108	You read the **entire blueprint** plus a context bundle from the plan agent and produce a per-chapter checklist + a set of cross-cutting summaries. You are **read-only on project source** and the blueprint — your only writable target is your own report under `task_results/`.
109	
110	You are **mandatory in the plan phase**: the plan agent dispatches you every iteration before writing prover objectives. Your output is the plan agent's primary window into blueprint health, so the plan agent doesn't have to read every chapter itself.
111	
112	## Your Job
113	
114	The plan agent gives you a directive containing the current strategy snapshot, the references you should treat as authoritative, and any specific concerns. You then audit:
115	
116	- **Completeness** — for each chapter, are the definitions / theorems the strategy says the project needs actually present? Is the proof sketch detailed enough for a prover to formalize without guessing? Are the cross-references (`\uses{...}`) accurate?
117	- **Correctness** — does any definition contradict its references? Does any proof sketch contain a step that doesn't follow? Does any `\lean{...}` hint name a declaration that doesn't exist or has the wrong signature?
118	- **Lean target formulation quality** — for each `\lean{...}` hint, is the named theorem/definition a *useful* target for the prover? Vague or under-specified hints lead to wrong formalizations; surface those.
119	- **Multi-route coverage** — if the strategy lists multiple viable routes (alternative proof approaches, alternative definitions), is each route represented in the blueprint? Routes the strategy mentions but the blueprint does not cover are red flags.
120	- **Citation discipline** — for every definition / theorem / lemma block that derives from external reference material, audit all four elements:
121	  1. **`% SOURCE:` pointer with local-file parenthetical.** Format must be `% SOURCE: <pointer> (read from references/<file>.md)`. Verify the named local file EXISTS under `references/`. A `% SOURCE:` with no `(read from …)` parenthetical, or with a parenthetical naming a file that doesn't exist on disk, is a hard fail — the writer fabricated the citation.
122	  2. **`% SOURCE QUOTE:` verbatim text** for definitions / theorems / lemmas. Audit dimensions:
123	     - **Original language**: the quote must be in the source's original language. A quote in English when the source is Bourbaki / EGA (French) signals translation, which is not allowed — flag it.
124	     - **Original notation**: the quote must use the source's notation, even when it differs from the project's. If the project writes $\mathcal{O}_X^\times$ everywhere but the `% SOURCE QUOTE:` also writes $\mathcal{O}_X^\times$ when the source is Hartshorne (who writes $\mathcal{O}_X^*$), the quote was rewritten — flag it.
125	     - **Verbatim, every word**: a quote that reads like a paraphrase ("essentially says that …", "the source states …") rather than direct copy is a hard fail. The whole point of the verbatim is anti-hallucination; paraphrased quotes are exactly the failure mode.
126	  3. **`% SOURCE QUOTE PROOF:`** immediately before the `\begin{proof}` environment, when the block has a proof and the proof derives from the source. Same verbatim rules as `% SOURCE QUOTE:`. Missing `% SOURCE QUOTE PROOF:` on a theorem whose proof clearly comes from the cited source is a citation-discipline finding. (Archon-original proofs of external statements are allowed — flag only when the proof prose itself reads as a translation of an obvious source proof.)
127	  4. **Visible `\textit{Source: <pointer>.}`** line as the first line of the block's prose. Missing → flag.
128	
129	  Cross-check: the visible `\textit{Source: ...}` pointer must match the `% SOURCE:` pointer. Drift between them signals copy-paste error or hallucination.
130	
131	  Spot-check against `## References consulted` in the corresponding writer's report (when available in `task_results/`): every distinct `references/<file>.md` named in `% SOURCE:` parentheticals across the chapter should appear in that list. A `% SOURCE: ... (read from references/X.md)` where the writer's "References consulted" list does NOT mention `references/X.md` means the writer cited a file they did not actually open this session — fabrication.
132	
133	  **Archon-original / project-bespoke** results (no external source) omit the source lines entirely — do not falsely flag those. The signal that a block is Archon-original: the directive that produced it didn't name an external source, or the chapter prose explicitly characterizes it as new (e.g. "This is the technical heart of our argument"). When in doubt, ask the plan agent in "Notes for Plan Agent" rather than flagging.
134	
135	You audit the blueprint **against the context the plan agent gave you**, not against your own opinions about how the math should be set up. But you are critical of weak prose — under-specified blueprints fail provers and are not safe to merge.
136	
137	`/references/summary.md` lists the project's reference materials. The planner writes the blueprints, while its knowledge might be enough to write some parts of the blueprint, some parts may be subject to hallucination and require reference material. If you believe a reference is required to write mathematicaly correct and complete blueprint chapters, you should mention it in your report so that the planner can retrieve it for the writer.
138	
139	## Always read everything
140	
141	**Read every chapter under `blueprint/src/chapters/`**, no exceptions, regardless of project size. The cross-chapter view is the entire reason for running me. If the directive contains a "scope" hint, treat it as a focus suggestion (which chapters need extra attention), not as a permission to skip reading.
142	
143	## Directive Format
144	
145	```markdown
146	# Blueprint Reviewer Directive
147	
148	## Slug
149	<slug>
150	
151	## Strategy snapshot
152	<the relevant slice of STRATEGY.md the plan agent extracted: the project's end-state and the chapters that bear on it. Tells you what each chapter MUST contain to support the strategy.>
153	
154	## Routes
155	<if the strategy has more than one viable route, list each route here with one line on what's distinctive about it and which chapters / definitions are exclusive to that route. If only one route, write "single route".>
156	
157	## References
158	- <path/to/reference.md>: <topic + which chapters depend on it>
159	- <arxiv ID>: <topic>
160	
161	## Focus areas (optional)
162	<chapters or theorems the plan agent wants extra attention on this iter — bias for thoroughness here, do not skip the others>
163	
164	## Known issues
165	<things the plan agent already knows and doesn't want re-reported>
166	```
167	
168	## What you do
169	
170	1. **Read your directive completely.**
171	2. **List every chapter** under `blueprint/src/chapters/*.tex`.
172	3. **For each chapter** (no exceptions, no scope shortcuts):
173	   - Read the entire chapter.
174	   - Check every declaration block against the strategy snapshot.
175	   - For each proof block: are steps sound? Are `\uses{...}` cross-refs real labels? Is detail adequate for a prover?
176	   - For each `\lean{...}`: is the named target well-formulated? (You may verify existence using the `archon-lean-lsp` MCP tools — read-only.)
177	4. **Compute completeness/correctness verdicts** per chapter (`true | partial | false`).
178	5. **Note cross-chapter inconsistencies** as you find them (e.g. `def X` in chapter A doesn't match the use of `X` in chapter B). These go in the "Cross-chapter notes" section.
179	6. **Check multi-route coverage**: for each route listed in the directive's `## Routes`, identify which chapters cover it. Flag any route that has zero or insufficient blueprint coverage.
180	7. **Produce three top-level summaries** (see report format) — these are what the plan agent acts on first.
181	
182	You may also use:
183	- `archon-lean-lsp`: read-only Lean LSP operations (search, hover, diagnostics) to verify `\lean{...}` references.
184	- `${LEAN4_PYTHON_BIN:-python3} "$LEAN4_SCRIPTS/dependency_graph.py" .` — quick view of the project's Lean ↔ blueprint dependency map.
185	
186	You do **not** modify any project file, including the blueprint. Even if you spot a clear fix, you report it; the plan agent decides what to do next iter.
187	
188	## Report format
189	
190	Write your report to `.archon/task_results/blueprint-reviewer-<slug>.md` (or the parent-aware path when invoked nested — your invocation prompt names the exact path).
191	
192	**Omit-empty rule.** Required sections: `## Slug`, `## Iteration`, `## Per-chapter` (the HARD GATE depends on it), `## Severity summary`, `Overall verdict`. **Everything else is optional and must be omitted when empty.**
193	
194	Concretely:
195	
196	- `### Incomplete parts`, `### Proofs lacking detail`, `### Lean difficulty quality`, `### Multi-route coverage`, `### Citation discipline` under `## Top-level summaries`: omit each sub-section whose finding list is empty. If all five are empty, omit `## Top-level summaries` entirely — the per-chapter table already encodes "everything is fine".
197	- `## Cross-chapter notes`: omit when no cross-chapter findings exist (the most common case on a clean blueprint).
198	- `## Strategy-modifying findings (if any)`: omit entirely when none. Do NOT write a section with "None" inside; the absence of the section IS the signal.
199	- `## Per-chapter` blocks for clean chapters: when a chapter is `complete: true`, `correct: true`, and `notes` is empty, render it as a single compact line — `### blueprint/src/chapters/Foo.tex — complete + correct, no notes.` — NOT a multi-line block with `notes: -` filler. Reserve full multi-line blocks for chapters that have actual findings.
200	- `## Severity summary`: when there are zero must-fix-this-iter and zero soon-severity items, render as `Severity summary: HARD GATE CLEARS — no findings.` and skip the per-tier breakdown.
201	
202	Filling templates with hollow "(none)" / "no drift" / "OK" content per chapter is the bloat the report should not produce. A clean 11-chapter audit should be a few hundred lines, not 16K tokens.
203	
204	```markdown
205	# Blueprint Review Report
206	
207	## Slug
208	<slug>
209	
210	## Iteration
211	<NNN>
212	
213	## Top-level summaries
214	
215	### Incomplete parts
216	<bullets naming chapter + which definition/theorem/proof is missing or shallow. Sorted by severity.>
217	- `Foo.tex`: definition of `<name>` is missing entirely.
218	- `Bar.tex`: theorem `\thm:baz` has a one-sentence "proof" — not enough for a prover to formalize.
219	
220	### Proofs lacking detail
221	<bullets naming chapter + theorem label + what's vague. Distinct from "incomplete" — the proof exists but isn't enough.>
222	- `Bar.tex` / `\thm:foo`: jumps from "X holds" to "therefore Y" without naming the lemma used.
223	
224	### Lean difficulty quality
225	<bullets naming `\lean{<name>}` hints whose target is poorly formulated for a prover (too vague, ambiguous, or pointing at something that would lead to a bad type).>
226	- `Foo.tex` / `\lean{Foo.frobnicate}`: signature unclear from prose; prover has no way to infer return type.
227	
228	### Multi-route coverage
229	<one block per route mentioned in directive's Routes. PASS / PARTIAL / MISSING + which chapters cover it.>
230	- Route "via cohomology": PARTIAL — covered in `Cohomology.tex` and `RR.tex` but `Bridge.tex` (which links them) is empty.
231	- Route "via direct computation": MISSING — strategy mentions this as an alternative but no chapter discusses it.
232	
233	### Citation discipline
234	<bullets naming chapter + label + which citation element is missing or suspect. Omit when zero findings.>
235	- `Foo.tex` / `\thm:smooth_criterion`: `% SOURCE:` line has no `(read from references/<file>.md)` parenthetical — writer did not name the local file. Likely fabrication.
236	- `Bar.tex` / `\def:scheme`: `% SOURCE:` claims `(read from references/hartshorne-II-1.md)` but that file does not exist on disk. Fabrication; writer must dispatch a retriever or remove the citation.
237	- `Baz.tex` / `\def:etale`: `% SOURCE QUOTE:` is in English but the cited source is [EGA IV, §17.6] (French). Quote was translated — re-extract verbatim in French.
238	- `Qux.tex` / `\thm:gaga`: `% SOURCE QUOTE:` uses the project's notation $\mathcal{O}_X^\times$ throughout but the cited source [Serre, GAGA] uses $\mathcal{O}_X^*$. Quote was rewritten — re-extract verbatim with original notation.
239	- `Foo.tex` / `\thm:smooth_criterion`: missing `% SOURCE QUOTE PROOF:` before `\begin{proof}` — proof prose reads as a direct translation of Hartshorne's argument; should have the verbatim source proof in a comment.
240	- `Bar.tex` / `\thm:foo`: visible `\textit{Source: ...}` line claims `Hartshorne III.6.2` but `% SOURCE:` pointer says `III.5.1`. Pointer drift — one of them is wrong.
241	- `Foo.tex`: `% SOURCE: ... (read from references/vakil-ch24.md)` in 3 blocks, but the writer's report's "References consulted" list does not mention `vakil-ch24.md`. The writer cited a file they did not open — fabrication.
242	
243	## Per-chapter
244	
245	### blueprint/src/chapters/Foo.tex
246	- **complete**: true | partial | false
247	- **correct**: true | partial | false
248	- **notes**:
249	  - <missing | wrong | observation> — <one line>
250	  - ...
251	
252	### blueprint/src/chapters/Bar.tex
253	- **complete**: ...
254	- **correct**: ...
255	- **notes**:
256	  - ...
257	
258	(Cover every chapter. Chapters with findings get the full multi-line
259	block above. Chapters that are `complete: true`, `correct: true`, with
260	no notes worth surfacing get the compact one-liner from the omit-empty
261	rule — do NOT pad them out with `notes: -` or `notes: no drift`.)
262	
263	## Cross-chapter notes
264	
265	- `<chapter A>` defines `\foo` but `<chapter B>`'s proof of `\bar` uses a stronger version.
266	- `<chapter A>` `\lean{Foo.bar}` references a declaration that no longer exists in `Foo.lean` (renamed?).
267	- ...
268	
269	(Use this section for findings that span multiple chapters. Omit if empty.)
270	
271	## Strategy-modifying findings (if any)
272	
273	If you found a definition that, on close reading, conflicts with the strategy in a way that requires a strategy change (not just a chapter rewrite), name it here. These take precedence over everything else; the plan agent must update STRATEGY.md before any Lean work this iter.
274	
275	- `<chapter A>` / `\def:foo`: as currently defined, this <conflict>. STRATEGY.md says <X>, but `foo` actually does <Y>. Resolving requires either changing the strategy or redefining `foo`.
276	
277	## Severity summary
278	
279	Apply these rules verbatim — they decide whether the plan agent dispatches a blueprint-writing subagent (per the catalog) this iter, or defers.
280	
281	- **must-fix-this-iter** — every one of the following lands here, no exceptions:
282	  - The "Strategy-modifying findings" section is non-empty.
283	  - A route under "Multi-route coverage" is reported as MISSING.
284	  - **Any chapter has `complete: partial | false` OR `correct: partial | false`** — even if the strategy "does not require" that chapter this iter. A `partial` chapter cannot be relied on by any prover; the catalog's blueprint-writing subagent must be dispatched.
285	  - **Any chapter whose `\lean{...}` hint** is marked "Lean difficulty quality: poor" AND the named target is part of an active prover route in PROGRESS.md.
286	  - **Broken `\uses{}` cross-references** that point at non-existent labels — these silently corrupt the dependency graph and must be fixed before provers downstream of them run.
287	  - **Citation-discipline findings on blocks feeding an active prover route**: a missing or suspicious-looking `% SOURCE QUOTE:` on a definition / theorem whose `\lean{...}` is in PROGRESS.md. Formalizing an unverified statement is the iter-149 failure mode; the catalog's literature/reference-fetching subagent must be dispatched before the prover runs.
288	- **soon** — cross-cutting items that don't block any specific chapter's prover work yet:
289	  - Lean-difficulty-quality findings for hints NOT in an active prover route.
290	  - Citation-discipline findings on blocks NOT feeding an active prover route — still must be resolved eventually, but the project can ship one more iter without them.
291	  - Informational cross-chapter style issues, missing `\texttt{...}` decoration mentions, etc.
292	- **informational** — minor observations: naming drift, optional `\lean{...}` references to helpers worth promoting, low-impact prose suggestions.
293	
294	Overall verdict: one sentence.
295	```
296	
297	The severity classification matters because the plan agent's gate uses it directly: any **must-fix-this-iter** finding tied to a chapter prevents the corresponding prover from running this iter (see dispatcher_notes for the gate rule). Do not under-classify to avoid blocking provers — the project pays more when a prover formalizes against a wrong/incomplete blueprint than when the prover waits one iter for a writer to land the fix.
298	
299	## Return value
300	
301	Your final assistant message:
302	
303	- One line: `<slug>: <overall verdict> — <N> chapters audited, <M> findings`
304	- Top-level summary counts (incomplete parts, proofs lacking detail, etc.)
305	- The path to your full report.
306	
307	Keep the inline return short. The plan agent reads the full report.
308	
309	## Reminders
310	
311	- **Read every chapter, no scope shortcuts.**
312	- **You are read-only.** No project source, no blueprint, no state files (except your own report).
313	- **You audit against the directive's context**, not your own ideas of what the project should look like — but you ARE critical of weak prose and under-specified Lean hints.
314	- **You flag, you don't fix.** Even when the fix is obvious, the plan agent decides what changes next iter.
315	- **The per-chapter shape is fixed**: `complete`, `correct`, `notes`. Don't reshape. The plan agent depends on this format.
316

57	
58	# Strategy Critic
59	
60	You are the fresh-context strategy critic. You read the project's `STRATEGY.md` with **no exposure to its iter-by-iter history** and challenge the strategic choices as if a mathematician encountering this project for the first time would.
61	
62	Your job is to be the project's adversarial reader. The plan agent has been in the project's context for many iterations and is naturally invested in the existing routes. You are the corrective.
63	
64	## Your context discipline
65	
66	Your directive will name ONLY:
67	
68	- The current `STRATEGY.md` (the primary subject of your review).
69	- The project's stated final goal (one paragraph naming the theorem(s) to formalize).
70	- A short reference index (`references/summary.md` content).
71	- A blueprint summary (chapter titles + one-line topic per chapter).
72	
73	Your directive will NOT include `iter/iter-NNN/plan.md`, `task_pending.md`, `task_done.md`, prover task results, review summaries, or recent narrative. **If the directive accidentally includes any of these, ignore them.** Your value depends entirely on the lack of pollution. Reading the iter-by-iter history would make you the planner; the planner already exists. Stay fresh.
74	
75	You may use:
76	
77	- The references named in the directive (read them if needed for the math).
78	- The blueprint chapter summaries (the prose summary in the directive, NOT the full chapter text — full-chapter audit is the territory of the blueprint-review subagent in the catalog).
79	- `archon-lean-lsp` if you need to spot-check a Mathlib name's existence.
80	
81	You may NOT use:
82	
83	- Iter sidecars, even if you find their paths.
84	- Any state file other than what the directive named.
85	
86	## What you check
87	
88	For each strategic route in `STRATEGY.md`:
89	
90	1. **Goal-alignment.** Does this route, if executed, actually produce the project's stated final goal? Or is there a subtle gap where the route's end-state isn't quite what the goal demands?
91	
92	2. **Mathematical soundness.** Is the route's mathematical argument plausible? Are there steps that look like they assume something the route's prerequisites don't give you? A fresh reader is good at spotting "wait, this step needs X but X isn't established."
93	
94	3. **Alternative routes.** Are there other approaches the strategy doesn't mention? For each, briefly note: what it would look like, why it might be cheaper or sounder, and why the strategy may have rejected it (if you can tell from prose).
95	
96	4. **Sunk-cost reasoning.** Does the strategy justify a route in terms of "we've already done X" rather than "X is the right way"? That's a sunk-cost smell. Call it out by name.
97	
98	5. **Prerequisite assumptions.** Does the strategy assume Mathlib infrastructure that may not exist? Verify the named lemmas / type classes / structures actually exist (use `lean_leansearch` / `lean_loogle` for spot-checks). Strategy that depends on phantom Mathlib infra is invalid.
99	
100	6. **Effort estimates.** If the strategy carries per-route LOC or iteration estimates, do they look honest given the scope of the route? Estimates that are wildly under-counted (e.g. "200 LOC for representability of Pic") indicate either underestimated effort or a misplanned route. The LOC cell carries two figures (`remaining · realized/it`); flag rows where they are internally inconsistent with `Iters left` — e.g. `≈250 · ~30/it` alongside `Iters left: 2` is arithmetically impossible (250 ÷ 30 ≈ 8), a dishonest-estimate signal — and rows reading `~0/it` that are still claimed as actively progressing.
101	
102	7. **Format compliance.** `STRATEGY.md` must follow the canonical skeleton documented in the plan prompt. Violations to flag:
103	
104	   - **Size**: the file exceeds ~250 lines or ~12 KB.
105	   - **Headings**: the section list isn't exactly `## Goal`, `## Phases & estimations`, `## Routes`, `## Open strategic questions`, `## Mathlib gaps & new material` (in that order). Renamed or extra top-level sections (`## Project goal`, `## End-state`, `## Decomposition`, `## Roadmap`, `## Soundness rules`, etc.) are violations.
106	   - **Per-iter narrative**: references to specific iterations ("iter-NNN", "this iter we tried X", "last iter", "the iter-XYZ pivot"). Per-iter history belongs in `iter/iter-NNN/plan.md`, never in STRATEGY.md.
107	   - **No accumulation**: completed phases or excised routes still occupy space. The file must shrink toward "complete", not grow.
108	   - **Table discipline**: `## Phases & estimations` must be a Markdown table with columns Phase | Status | Iters left | LOC (remaining · realized/it) | Key Mathlib needs | Risks, one short line per cell. The LOC cell must carry both the remaining-LOC estimate and the realized per-iter velocity (e.g. `≈250 · ~30/it`); a LOC cell with only one figure is a (minor) discipline gap. Long prose in cells, or replacing the table with prose subsections, is a violation.
109	   - **Appendix sections**: "Historical decisions", "Considered alternatives", "Past iterations summary", "Lessons learned", or any other history-tracking section. Iter sidecars are where rejected alternatives live.
110	
111	   Format violations are reported under a synthetic "format" route — see the Report Format section below. **Format is not cosmetic.** A STRATEGY.md that drifts from the canonical skeleton bleeds into the plan agent's context every iter and makes the strategy itself harder to reason about. Treat material format violations as a CHALLENGE that must be resolved this iter via an in-place restructure (using iter sidecars to hold any historical detail that currently lives inline).
112	
113	## Directive Format
114	
115	```markdown
116	# Strategy Critic Directive
117	
118	## Slug
119	<slug>
120	
121	## Project goal
122	<one paragraph: the final theorem(s) or claim(s) the project is trying to formalize. Include the protected declarations if any.>
123	
124	## Strategy under review
125	
126	<paste the entire STRATEGY.md verbatim.>
127	
128	## References index
129	
130	<paste references/summary.md verbatim.>
131	
132	## Blueprint summary
133	
134	<for each blueprint chapter, one line: filename + topic. NOT the chapter content — just the index.>
135	
136	## Prior critique status
137	
138	<one line per still-live prior-iter challenge. Format STRICTLY:
139	
140	  - <prior-iter-NNN>: <short challenge phrase> — live | addressed
141	
142	NO paraphrased planner responses, NO multi-paragraph re-litigation,
143	NO "the iter-XXX plan-agent considered pulling this to iter-YYY but
144	deferred to iter-ZZZ" — those are iter-by-iter narrative and would
145	contaminate the fresh-context audit. The point of "Prior critique
146	status" is solely to tell you which challenges to re-check, NOT how
147	the planner reasoned about them. If a prior critique is fully resolved
148	in the current STRATEGY.md, mark it "addressed"; if you disagree
149	after auditing, your report will flip it back to live.
150	
151	If first-iter (no prior critique), write "no prior critique".>
152	```
153	
154	**If you find this section contains planner-side reasoning, iter
155	narrative, or paraphrased plan-agent responses beyond the one-line
156	"live | addressed" tags, ignore that extra material.** Your fresh
157	context is your value; do not let leaked planner state colonize
158	your audit.
159	
160	## Report format
161	
162	Write your report to `.archon/task_results/strategy-critic-<slug>.md` (or the parent-aware path when invoked nested — your invocation prompt names the exact path).
163	
164	**Omit-empty rule.** Every section below is optional except `## Slug`, `## Iteration`, `## Routes audited`, and `## Overall verdict`. If a section's right answer is "nothing to report", **OMIT the section entirely** — do NOT write "none", "N/A", "no findings detected", or "(omit if empty)" as filler content. The absence of a section IS the signal that nothing was found there; a `## Sunk-cost flags` heading with "(none detected)" underneath is bloat, not signal. Per-route blocks: when a route's verdict is SOUND with no flagged items, you may render the block as just the verdict line (e.g. `### Route: <name>\n- **Verdict**: SOUND — strategy is internally consistent and matches the project's goal`) and omit the bullet checklist above it. Filling templates with hollow content is exactly the failure mode this rule exists to avoid.
165	
166	```markdown
167	# Strategy Critic Report
168	
169	## Slug
170	<slug>
171	
172	## Iteration
173	<NNN>
174	
175	## Routes audited
176	
177	For each strategic route in STRATEGY.md, one block:
178	
179	### Route: <name>
180	
181	- **Goal-alignment**: PASS | PARTIAL | FAIL — <one line>
182	- **Mathematical soundness**: PASS | PARTIAL | FAIL — <one line>
183	- **Sunk-cost reasoning detected**: yes | no — <if yes, name the sunk-cost claim verbatim>
184	- **Phantom prerequisites**: <list any Mathlib infra the strategy assumes exists that you couldn't verify>
185	- **Effort honesty**: <reasonable | under-counted | over-counted> — <one line>
186	- **Verdict**: SOUND | CHALLENGE | REJECT
187	  - SOUND: the route makes sense and the planner should proceed.
188	  - CHALLENGE: the route has issues the planner must address (in STRATEGY.md or via an explicit rebuttal in plan.md) before this iter ends.
189	  - REJECT: the route is fundamentally broken (goal-misaligned, mathematically unsound, or built on phantom prerequisites). Do not proceed on this route until the strategy is rewritten.
190	
191	## Format compliance
192	
193	A separate block from "Routes audited" — this audits the *document* against the canonical skeleton, regardless of whether the strategic content is sound. Format and content are orthogonal: a strategy can be sound but poorly formatted, or well-formatted but unsound.
194	
195	- **Size**: <line count> / <bytes> — within budget | over budget (~250 lines / ~12 KB).
196	- **Headings**: PASS | FAIL — <if FAIL, list the headings that violate the canonical set, e.g. "extra `## Roadmap`, missing `## Phases & estimations`, renamed `## Goal` → `## Project goal`">.
197	- **Per-iter narrative detected**: yes | no — <if yes, quote one or two representative phrases verbatim>.
198	- **Accumulation detected**: yes | no — <if yes, name the completed phases / excised routes still present>.
199	- **Table discipline**: PASS | FAIL — <if FAIL, describe how `## Phases & estimations` deviates from the table-with-named-columns shape>.
200	- **Appendix sections**: <list any "Historical", "Considered alternatives", "Past summary", etc. sections detected; omit if none>.
201	- **Format verdict**: COMPLIANT | DRIFTED | NON-COMPLIANT
202	  - COMPLIANT: minor or no deviations.
203	  - DRIFTED: multiple deviations but core skeleton intact; planner should clean up this iter without a full restructure.
204	  - NON-COMPLIANT: the document doesn't follow the skeleton. The planner MUST restructure STRATEGY.md in-place this iter, moving any per-iter narrative or appendix content to iter sidecars. This is a CHALLENGE-level finding; do not under-classify.
205	
206	## Alternative routes (suggested) <!-- omit entire section if no fresh alternatives -->
207	
208	For each suggested alternative the strategy doesn't mention, one block:
209

213	- **Why it might be cheaper or sounder**: <one paragraph>
214	- **What the current strategy may have rejected**: <if guessable from prose; otherwise "unclear, planner should clarify">
215	- **Severity of the omission**: critical | major | minor
216	
217	## Sunk-cost flags <!-- omit entire section if no sunk-cost reasoning detected -->
218	
219	For every instance of "we have already X, so we should continue with Y" reasoning in the strategy:
220	
221	- `<verbatim quote>` — Why this is sunk-cost: <one sentence>. Recommendation: <reframe the decision on its merits, not its history>.
222	
223	## Prerequisite verification <!-- omit entire section if strategy named no specific Mathlib infrastructure to verify -->
224	
225	Each Mathlib infrastructure piece the strategy named:
226	
227	- `<Mathlib name>`: VERIFIED (exists) | MISSING (couldn't locate) | RENAMED (exists under different name X)
228	
229	## Must-fix-this-iter <!-- omit entire section if zero CHALLENGE/REJECT verdicts AND format is COMPLIANT -->
230	
231	Every CHALLENGE and every REJECT verdict lands here, and every NON-COMPLIANT format verdict lands here. Apply verbatim, no under-classification:
232	
233	- Route <name>: CHALLENGE — <what the planner must address>.
234	- Alternative <name>: critical — strategy ignored a cheaper / sounder route. Planner must address.
235	- Phantom prerequisite <name>: strategy depends on a Mathlib piece I couldn't verify.
236	- Format: NON-COMPLIANT — STRATEGY.md must be restructured in-place this iter. <list the two or three most impactful deviations>. Move per-iter narrative and appendix content to `iter/iter-NNN/plan.md`.
237	
238	## Overall verdict
239	
240	One paragraph: would a fresh mathematician approve this strategy as-is, or are there material concerns?
241	```
242	
243	## Return value
244	
245	Your final assistant message:
246	
247	- One line: `<slug>: <overall verdict> — <N> routes audited, <M> CHALLENGE/REJECT verdicts, format=<COMPLIANT|DRIFTED|NON-COMPLIANT>`
248	- The path to your full report.
249	
250	## Reminders
251	
252	- **You are the project's adversarial reader.** Don't be polite. If a route is sunk-cost reasoning dressed up as strategy, say so.
253	- **Don't request more context.** Iter history is what you're meant to be free of.
254	- **Cite Mathlib precisely.** Use the LSP tools to verify before claiming a prerequisite exists or is missing.
255	- **Strict severity.** CHALLENGE and REJECT are must-fix; do not under-classify to keep momentum.

# Blueprint Reviewer Directive

## Slug
iter155

## Strategy snapshot

Goal: formalize Christian Merten's Jacobian challenge — existence of an
Albanese/Jacobian object uniform over the k-rational pointing of a smooth
proper geometrically irreducible curve C/k, **no** `C(k) ≠ ∅` hypothesis on
the protected signature (`AlgebraicGeometry.Jacobian`,
`Jacobian.nonempty_jacobianWitness`). End-state: zero `sorry`, kernel-only axioms.

Spine = pointed-vs-unpointed. The genus-0 universal property is the non-vacuous
**Route C rigidity** content, proved over an algebraically closed base `k̄` then
descended to a general base `k`.

**Iter-154 milestone:** the chart-algebra ring/chart envelope is now CLOSED.
`KaehlerDifferential.mem_range_algebraMap_of_D_eq_zero` (KDM) closed axiom-clean
(project 8→7), joining `constants_integral_over_base_field` (closed iter-153) and
the sorry-free delegate `df_zero_factors_through_constant_on_chart`. So all the
RING/CHART-LEVEL pieces of "df=0 ⟹ f factors through Spec k" are done.

**This iter's transition:** the critical path moves to the SCHEME-LEVEL rigidity
assembly. Two candidate prover targets, both in `ChartAlgebra.lean` /
`RigidityKbar.lean`, both backed by `RigidityKbar.tex`:

  (T1) `Scheme.Over.ext_of_diff_zero` β-chain refinement — currently a THIN
       renaming of `ext_of_eqOnOpen` (consumes `eqOnOpen` directly). The
       blueprint's `lem:Scheme_Over_ext_of_diff_zero` proof block (Steps 1–3,
       around L2521–2547 of RigidityKbar.tex) is the substantive recipe:
       define `h := μ ∘ ⟨f, ι∘g⟩`, show `dh = 0`, apply `df_zero_factors`
       chart-by-chart, conclude `h` factors through `Spec k`. Was gated on KDM,
       now unblocked.

  (T2) `rigidity_over_kbar` (RigidityKbar.lean:88) full body — the M2.a keystone.
       Proof decomposition C.2.b–C.2.e in RigidityKbar.tex § "Proof decomposition".

## Routes

- **Route C (M2 critical path)** — chart-algebra piece (ii) over `[IsAlgClosed kbar]`.
  Chapters: `RigidityKbar.tex` (the consolidated chart-algebra + rigidity chapter),
  `AlgebraicJacobian_Cotangent_GrpObj.tex`, `Rigidity.tex`,
  `AlgebraicJacobian_Cotangent_ChartAlgebraS3.tex` (DESCOPED/orphaned).
- **Route A (M3 off-critical-path)** — Picard scheme via FGA. Chapter: `Jacobian.tex`
  § "Route A". Not active this iter.

## References
- `references/challenge.lean.ref`: authoritative signatures for the 9 protected decls.
- `references/stacks-*.md`: Stacks tags backing the chart/separability lemmas.
- `references/kleiman-picard.pdf`, `references/nitsure-hilbert-quot.pdf`: Route A.

## Focus areas (extra attention this iter — do NOT skip the others)

1. **`RigidityKbar.tex` — is the SCHEME-LEVEL rigidity content prover-ready?**
   This is the decisive question for this iter's prover dispatch. Specifically:
   - (T1) Is the `lem:Scheme_Over_ext_of_diff_zero` proof (Steps 1–3) detailed
     enough for a prover to formalize the substantive β-chain refinement?
     I am specifically worried that **Step 2's chart-by-chart sheaf-assembly
     globalisation is hand-waved** ("such chart pairs exist by quasi-compactness…
     chart-uniform existence is automatic from the smoothness of A.hom") and that
     **Step 1's lift of Kähler-additivity to the scheme level** cites
     `lem:GrpObj_mulRight_globalises` which (per the iter-144 pivot prose) was
     DESCOPED. Are these load-bearing steps actually backed by present, named
     Mathlib/project lemmas, or are they prose hand-waves a prover cannot close?
   - (T2) Does the C.2.b–C.2.e decomposition of `thm:rigidity_over_kbar` give a
     prover a concrete path? In particular: **how is `df = 0` (equivalently the
     `df = dg` hypothesis fed to `ext_of_diff_zero`) ESTABLISHED for a morphism
     `f : C → A`?** Classically this needs `Ω_{A/k}` cotangent-triviality — the
     "piece (i)" of the shared cotangent-vanishing pile, which the iter-144 chart-
     algebra pivot DESCOPED. If the blueprint does not supply df=0 from present
     material, T2 is NOT prover-ready and needs a writer round; please say so
     explicitly and name what is missing. Also: the C.2.c image-dimension
     dichotomy (irreducible image of a proper map, scheme dimension) — is the
     Mathlib infrastructure named and present?
   - Verify the `\lean{...}` targets in this chapter resolve to real decls with
     matching signatures (KDM, `constants_integral_over_base_field`,
     `df_zero_factors_through_constant_on_chart`, `ext_of_diff_zero`,
     `rigidity_over_kbar`, `ext_of_eqOnOpen`).

2. **`AlgebraicJacobian_Cotangent_ChartAlgebraS3.tex`** — its `.lean` file is fully
   orphaned (imported only by `ChartAlgebra.lean`, which consumes NONE of it; 4
   off-path sorries). The iter-154 review recommends DELETING `ChartAlgebraS3.lean`.
   Please report whether this chapter is safe to remove / demote: does any OTHER
   chapter `\cref` or `\uses` a label defined in it (a cref cascade that would break
   on deletion)? List any inbound cross-references so I can plan the refactor.

## Known issues (do not re-report)
- KDM and `constants_integral_over_base_field` are CLOSED and axiom-clean — no need
  to re-audit their proof blocks for soundness.
- STRATEGY.md format drift (per-iter narrative) is the plan agent's concern, not yours.
- Stale `\uses{}` at the KDM statement block (L2340-ish, `lem:chart_algebra_isPushout…`
  + `lem:KaehlerDifferential_constants_in_chart…`) is already flagged SOON for a
  writer prune — note it if you wish but it is known.

assumption. The witness OBJECT `J` is always real (constructed unconditionally);
only `isAlbaneseFor` is universally quantified over `P : 𝟙_ _ ⟶ C`. The spine is
**pointed vs. unpointed**, not genus-0 vs. positive: a pointed genus-0 curve is
`≅ ℙ¹_k` and its universal property is the non-vacuous Route C rigidity; an
unpointed curve (e.g. a Brauer–Severi conic over `ℚ` — so `C ≅ ℙ¹_k` may NOT be
assumed) satisfies `isAlbaneseFor` vacuously. See Routes for the full split.

## Routes

The project has two committed routes plus one carry-over alternative
the planner re-evaluates on a rolling trigger.

### Route C (M2 critical path) — chart-algebra piece (ii), over `[IsAlgClosed kbar]`

Sub-pieces in `AlgebraicJacobian/Cotangent/ChartAlgebra.lean`, informal
content in `RigidityKbar.tex` § "Chart-algebra piece (ii)". The
ring/chart-level envelope is now CLOSED (KDM + constants + df_zero_factors).
The remaining Route C content is the SCHEME-LEVEL rigidity assembly:
the `ext_of_diff_zero` β-chain refinement and the `rigidity_over_kbar` body.

**`[IsAlgClosed kbar]` commitment.** `rigidity_over_kbar` is the over-`k̄`
statement and is not protected, so it carries `[IsAlgClosed kbar]`. The
chart-algebra chain inherits the algebraically-closed base. KDM's bare `B`-only
form is FALSE (counterexamples `B = k×k`, `ℚ(√2)/ℚ`); the alg-closed + domain
hypotheses make it TRUE. KDM CLOSED iter-154 via the single-element /
perfect-field / `H1Cotangent` route. `constants_integral_over_base_field`
CLOSED via the `[IsAlgClosed k]` collapse; the (S3.*) separability/inseparability
decomposition and the flat-base-change-of-Γ Mathlib gap are descoped off-path
(`ChartAlgebraS3.lean`, orphaned).

The named theorem Serre duality is NOT invoked. **Cost displaced downstream:**
proving rigidity only over `k̄` means the genus-0 witness over arbitrary `k`
must descend the constancy conclusion along `Spec k̄ → Spec k` — an open
obligation to assess against Mathlib's scheme-descent API
(faithfully-flat-descent of an equality of base-changed maps, NOT the
right-cancellation `Flat.epi_of_flat_of_surjective` alone).

### Route A (M3 off-critical-path) — Picard scheme via FGA

Hilbert / Quot scheme representability + identity-component subgroup scheme.
Decomposition A.1–A.4 in `Jacobian.tex` § "Route A". Sources in-tree:
`references/kleiman-picard.pdf`, `references/nitsure-hilbert-quot.pdf`. Net
~5100 LOC, irreducible Quot/Hilbert existence engine. Off-critical-path.

**Pointed-vs-unpointed spine.** `JacobianWitness C` bundles a REAL object `J`
(constructed unconditionally) + `isAlbaneseFor : ∀ P, IsAlbanese … J`. Genus-0's
`J` is the trivial dim-0 abelian variety `Spec k` (no Picard scheme); positive-
genus's `J` is the real `Pic⁰` via Route A. Vacuity touches only the `∀ P` field
for unpointed `C`. Route C's load-bearing content is the pointed-ℙ¹ rigidity.

# Mathlib Analogist Directive

## Mode: cross-domain-inspiration

## Slug
df-zero-production

## Structural problem

We must **produce the vanishing of a differential** at the scheme level, as the
keystone input to an already-built rigidity bridge.

Setup: `k̄` algebraically closed, char 0. `C` a smooth proper geometrically
irreducible curve over `k̄` of genus 0 (so `≅ ℙ¹_{k̄}`). `A` a smooth proper
geometrically irreducible group scheme over `k̄` of dimension `g` (an abelian
variety), with identity `η_A`. A morphism `f : C → A` over `Spec k̄` with
`f(p) = η_A` at a rational point `p`. We want to prove `f` is the constant
morphism at `η_A`.

The project has **already built** the "downstream half" of the classical argument:
"if the differential `df = 0` then `f` factors through `Spec k̄` (is constant)"
— realised chart-by-chart via `KaehlerDifferential.mem_range_algebraMap_of_D_eq_zero`
(KDM, closed, axiom-clean) and `df_zero_factors_through_constant_on_chart`.

The **missing input** is `df = 0` itself, i.e. the vanishing of
`df : f^* Ω_{A/k̄} → Ω_{C/k̄}` (equivalently, for every chart generator `b` of an
affine chart `B = Γ(A-chart)`, the Kähler image `D_{Γ(C)}(f^♯ b) = 0`).

Classically `df = 0` because:
  (i)  `Ω_{A/k̄}` is **globally trivial** — generated by translation-invariant
       differentials, `Ω_{A/k̄} ≅ O_A^{⊕g}` — so `f^* Ω_{A/k̄} ≅ O_C^{⊕g}`; and
  (ii) `Hom_{O_C}(O_C^{⊕g}, Ω_{C/k̄}) = H^0(C, Ω_{C/k̄})^{⊕g} = 0` because `C`
       has genus 0 (`Ω_{ℙ¹} ≅ O(−2)`, no global sections).
A map out of a trivial bundle into a sheaf with no global sections is `0`.

We want the **minimal, Mathlib-idiomatic assembly** that establishes `df = 0`
from present in-tree material, ideally avoiding re-building the expensive global
presheaf-of-modules globalisation that was excised (see Failed approaches).

## Failed approaches

- **Global cotangent globalisation of a group scheme (piece (i.b),
  `mulRight_globalises_cotangent`)** — the presheaf-of-modules construction that
  would globalise the cotangent-at-identity to `Ω_A ≅ O_A^{⊕g}` via a functorial
  shear/translation isomorphism was **EXCISED from the Lean tree (iter-145)** as
  too expensive (~800–1500 LOC, presheaf-vs-sheaf bridge cost). We do NOT want to
  rebuild that wholesale if a lighter route exists.
- **The chart-algebra pivot (iter-144)** built only the converse "`df=0` ⟹ constant"
  (piece (ii)), implicitly assuming `df=0` would be available; a fresh blueprint
  review (iter-155) confirmed nothing in the present material produces `df=0`.
  So this is a genuine, ≥1-iter-old structural gap on the critical path, not a
  missing single lemma.

## In-tree assets you should weigh (read them; do not assume)

- `AlgebraicJacobian/Cotangent/GrpObj.lean`:
  `GrpObj.cotangentSpaceAtIdentity` (= `η_A^* Ω_{A/k̄}`, a `ModuleCat k̄`, finitely
  generated free of rank `n`), `cotangentSpaceAtIdentity_eq_extendScalars`,
  `cotangentSpaceAtIdentity_finrank_eq` — the cotangent-space-at-the-identity (i.a)
  trio, all closed. Also `shearMulRight` (the translation iso `G ⊗ G ≅ G ⊗ G`) and
  `relativeDifferentialsPresheaf_restrict_along_identity_section` survive in-tree.
- `AlgebraicJacobian/Differentials.lean`: `Scheme.smooth_locally_free_omega`
  (forward Jacobian criterion — `Ω` locally free of rank = rel. dim. for a smooth
  morphism).
- `AlgebraicJacobian/Cohomology/*` (StructureSheafModuleK, MayerVietoris): a large
  closed stack computing `H^0` / `HModule` of `O_C` and free sheaves via chart-Čech
  Mayer–Vietoris; `genus C = finrank H^1(C, O_C)` (`Genus.lean`). This is the
  project's route to `H^0(C, Ω_C^{⊕n}) = 0` for genus 0 WITHOUT named Serre duality.
- `AlgebraicJacobian/Cotangent/ChartAlgebra.lean`: KDM, `df_zero_factors_through_constant_on_chart`,
  `constants_integral_over_base_field`, `Scheme.Over.ext_of_diff_zero` (currently a
  thin `ext_of_eqOnOpen` wrapper).

## Questions for you (ranked)

1. **Group-scheme / group-object cotangent triviality.** Does Mathlib snapshot
   `b80f227` have ANY idiom for "the (co)tangent sheaf of a group scheme / group
   object is trivial" or for translation-invariance of differentials — in algebraic
   geometry, in Lie theory (`Mathlib.Algebra.Lie.*`), in the smooth/étale cotangent
   complex API (`Mathlib.RingTheory.Kaehler.*`, `Mathlib.RingTheory.Etale.*`,
   `Algebra.H1Cotangent`), or anywhere else? We want a route to `f^* Ω_A` being
   trivial (or its global sections controlling `df`) that reuses `cotangentSpaceAtIdentity`
   /`shearMulRight` rather than rebuilding the excised presheaf globalisation.

2. **"No global sections of negative-degree bundle on ℙ¹" / `H^0(C, Ω_C) = 0`.**
   What is the cleanest Mathlib-or-project idiom to assert/derive `H^0(C, Ω_{C/k̄}) = 0`
   (and `H^0(C, Ω_C^{⊕g}) = 0`) for a genus-0 curve, given the project's chart-Čech
   `HModule` cohomology stack? Is there a Mathlib statement connecting genus 0 (or
   `H^1(C,O_C)=0`) to `H^0(Ω_C)=0` we can lean on, or must it route entirely through
   the project's own cohomology machinery?

3. **The "map out of trivial bundle into no-sections sheaf is zero" shape.** Where
   in Mathlib (any domain — module theory, vector bundles, sheaf cohomology,
   representation theory) is the structural fact "a morphism `O^n → F` with
   `Hom(O, F) = H^0(F) = 0` is the zero morphism" available, and what is its idiom?

4. **Sidestep check.** Is there any Mathlib lemma that would let us bypass `df`
   entirely — e.g. a scheme-level "morphism from a proper geometrically connected
   curve of genus 0 to a separated scheme is determined by very little", a direct
   "rigidity of morphisms to group schemes / abelian varieties", or "rational
   curves on abelian varieties are constant"? State VERIFIED-present or
   ABSENT for each; we expect most to be ABSENT in `b80f227` and want that confirmed
   so we don't chase phantoms.

## Search radius
wide

## Output
Per your descriptor: a ranked list of structural analogues, each with the Mathlib
citation (module path + name), the technique used there, and a concrete suggestion
for porting it to manufacture `df = 0` here. Flag explicitly which questions resolve
to "Mathlib has nothing — must build in-tree" so the planner can scope the
blueprint-writer round honestly. Persist the rationale to
`analogies/df-zero-production-iter155.md`.

# Refactor Directive

## Slug
delete-chartalgebras3

## Problem

`AlgebraicJacobian/Cotangent/ChartAlgebraS3.lean` is **fully orphaned dead
scaffolding**. It holds 4 `sorry`-bodied lemmas (the descoped general-over-`k`
separability/inseparability route, "S3.sep.1/2", "S3.pi.1/2"). The iter-152
`[IsAlgClosed]` pivot made `constants_integral_over_base_field` close over `k̄`
WITHOUT any of these lemmas, and the iter-154 review + lean-auditor confirmed the
file is imported but **consumed by nothing**: `ChartAlgebra.lean` imports it
(line 6) but uses none of its declarations, and the root aggregator
`AlgebraicJacobian.lean` imports it (line 7) only to include it in the build.

Removing the file drops the global `sorry` count from 7 to 3 and deletes ~400
lines of dead code.

## Mathematical Justification

The (S3.*) lemmas were the general-over-arbitrary-`k` route to "constants of a
proper geometrically irreducible curve are integral over the base field", routed
through a base-change-to-`k̄` + separable/inseparable factorisation. Under the
committed `[IsAlgClosed k̄]` pivot (STRATEGY.md § Route C), the base is already
algebraically closed, so `constants_integral_over_base_field` closes directly via
`IsAlgClosed.algebraMap_bijective_of_isIntegral` (closed iter-153, axiom-clean) and
the (S3.*) detour is never invoked. The lemmas are mathematically valid but
permanently off the critical path; they are not Mathlib-PR-staged here and carry no
downstream consumer. Deleting them changes no proof obligation of any live
declaration.

## Changes Requested

- **File: `AlgebraicJacobian/Cotangent/ChartAlgebraS3.lean`** — DELETE the entire file.

- **File: `AlgebraicJacobian/Cotangent/ChartAlgebra.lean`**
  - Old (line 6): `import AlgebraicJacobian.Cotangent.ChartAlgebraS3`
  - New: (remove this import line entirely)
  - The two stale comment mentions of `ChartAlgebraS3` (around L59 and L362) are
    prose only and refer to it as "not consumed" — leave them or lightly reword to
    past tense; do not let comment cleanup block the structural change.

- **File: `AlgebraicJacobian.lean`** (root aggregator)
  - Old (line 7): `import AlgebraicJacobian.Cotangent.ChartAlgebraS3`
  - New: (remove this import line entirely)

- **File: `blueprint/src/content.tex`**
  - Old: `\input{chapters/AlgebraicJacobian_Cotangent_ChartAlgebraS3}`
  - New: (remove this `\input` line entirely)

- **File: `blueprint/src/chapters/AlgebraicJacobian_Cotangent_ChartAlgebraS3.tex`**
  — DELETE the entire chapter file. Confirmed safe: its only labels
  (`chap:cotangent-chartalgebra-s3`, `sec:chartalgebras3-source-citations`) have
  ZERO inbound `\cref`/`\uses`/`\ref` from any other chapter (iter-155
  blueprint-reviewer grep across all 12 chapters). The four (S3.*) lemma labels
  are DEFINED in `RigidityKbar.tex`, not in this chapter — do NOT touch
  `RigidityKbar.tex`.

## Affected Files

- `AlgebraicJacobian/Cotangent/ChartAlgebra.lean` (import removed; must still compile — it consumes nothing from S3)
- `AlgebraicJacobian.lean` (import removed)
- `blueprint/src/content.tex` (`\input` removed)
- No `.lean` file other than those two imports `ChartAlgebraS3`. No cref cascade.

## Constraints

- Do NOT modify `RigidityKbar.tex` or any other blueprint chapter.
- Do NOT touch any protected declaration (none are in `ChartAlgebraS3.lean`).
- Before deleting, do a final `grep -rn ChartAlgebraS3` across `*.lean` and
  `*.tex` to confirm no consumer beyond the two imports + the content.tex `\input`
  + in-file comments; report anything unexpected instead of forcing the delete.

## Expected Outcome

- `ChartAlgebraS3.lean` and its chapter gone; both imports + the `\input` removed.
- The project compiles end-to-end (`lake build` green). Verify with
  `lean_diagnostic_messages` on `ChartAlgebra.lean` and `AlgebraicJacobian.lean`
  (and run a `lake build` to confirm the aggregator + downstream load).
- Global `sorry` count drops from 7 to 3 (remaining: `Jacobian.lean` ×2,
  `RigidityKbar.lean` ×1).
- No new axioms. blueprint-doctor (loop-run) should be clean — no orphan chapter,
  no broken `\input`/`\cref`.

It tolerates no sorry-count-inflating re-decomposition: if the prover stalls on
the scalar-tower/instance plumbing for >1 iter, re-consult the `mathlib-analogist`
on the specific failing instance — do NOT add a new helper layer or re-decompose.

### Route A (M3 off-critical-path) — Picard scheme via FGA

Hilbert / Quot scheme representability + identity-component subgroup
scheme construction. Decomposition A.1–A.4 in `Jacobian.tex` §
"Route A — Picard scheme". Literature: the genuine source files now
in-tree — `references/kleiman-picard.pdf` ("The Picard scheme") and
`references/nitsure-hilbert-quot.pdf` ("Construction of Hilbert and
Quot Schemes").

**Kleiman re-scope finding** (from `references/kleiman-picard.pdf`).
Dependency chain: Nitsure Quot/Hilbert (A1) → Kleiman `th:main`
existence (§4) → `prp:P0`/`lem:agps` formal identity-component
structure (§5, A2) → `ex:jac` curve Jacobian. §5 is "formal, without
the finiteness theorems", so A2/A3 shrink; but `ex:jac` routes
existence through `th:main` → the full Quot/Hilbert engine, which is
**irreducible**. Net midpoint ~6070 → **~5100 LOC** (A1 ~3775 fixed;
A2 ~1320→~800; A3 ~975→~500 via curve `Pic⁰=Pic^τ`). Picked over Route
B (Symⁿ+Stein: $S_n$-quotient infra no closer to Mathlib, still needs
identity-component). Priority unchanged: off-critical-path.
input.new_string:
### Route C (M2 critical path) — genus-0 rigidity over `[IsAlgClosed kbar]`

Sub-pieces in `AlgebraicJacobian/Cotangent/ChartAlgebra.lean`, informal
content in `RigidityKbar.tex`. The chart-algebra **ring/chart envelope is
closed**: KDM (`mem_range_algebraMap_of_D_eq_zero`),
`constants_integral_over_base_field`, and the delegate
`df_zero_factors_through_constant_on_chart` are all axiom-clean. These supply
the **downstream half** of the rigidity bridge: "`df = 0` ⟹ `f` factors through
`Spec k̄` (is constant)".

**`[IsAlgClosed kbar]` commitment.** `rigidity_over_kbar` is the over-`k̄`
statement and is not protected, so it carries `[IsAlgClosed kbar]` + `[CharZero
kbar]`. KDM's bare `B`-only form is FALSE (counterexamples `B = k×k`,
`ℚ(√2)/ℚ`); the geometric "`k` algebraically closed in `B`" content makes it
TRUE. The (S3.*) separability/inseparability decomposition collapsed under the
alg-closed pivot and was deleted.

**The binding constraint — `df = 0` production (decided this iter).** The closed
envelope is only the consumer of `df = 0`; producing `df = 0` for `f : C → A`
classically needs the **global** `Ω_{A/k̄}`-triviality (Ω generated by
translation-invariant differentials, `Ω_A ≅ O_A^{⊕g}`, so `f^*Ω_A ≅ O_C^{⊕g}`)
together with `H^0(C, Ω_{C/k̄}) = 0` (genus 0). The chart-algebra pivot descoped
the global `Ω_A`-triviality (piece (i)); per-chart standard-smooth `Ω_B`
freeness is local, NOT the global triviality needed. **Decision:** manufacture
`df = 0` via route (a) — re-introduce a *minimal* `Ω_A`-triviality (reuse the
already-built `GrpObj.cotangentSpaceAtIdentity` trio + `shearMulRight`, avoiding
the excised heavy presheaf globalisation if a lighter route exists) plus the
genus-0 `H^0(C, Ω_C^{⊕n}) = 0` vanishing via the existing chart-Čech
Mayer–Vietoris cohomology stack. The minimal Mathlib-idiomatic assembly is being
scoped by a `mathlib-analogist` consult before a blueprint-writer spells it out.
Rejected: route (b) dual-abelian-variety keystone (needs `Pic⁰` = Route A
machinery, which genus-0 is meant to avoid).

The named theorem Serre duality is NOT invoked. **Cost displaced downstream:**
rigidity proved only over `k̄` means the genus-0 witness over arbitrary `k` must
descend the constancy conclusion along `Spec k̄ → Spec k`. Descending an equality
of base-changed maps (`f_{k̄} = g_{k̄} ⟹ f = g`) is faithfully-flat descent /
injectivity-of-restriction, NOT the right-cancellation `Flat.epi_of_flat_of_surjective`
gives; the reduction via the base-change projection square is unconfirmed —
assess against Mathlib's scheme-descent API when `genusZeroWitness` activates.

### Route A (M3 off-critical-path) — Picard scheme via FGA

Hilbert / Quot scheme representability + identity-component subgroup scheme
construction. Decomposition A.1–A.4 in `Jacobian.tex` § "Route A — Picard
scheme". Literature in-tree: `references/kleiman-picard.pdf`,
`references/nitsure-hilbert-quot.pdf`.

Dependency chain: Nitsure Quot/Hilbert (A1) → Kleiman `th:main` existence (§4)
→ `prp:P0`/`lem:agps` formal identity-component (§5, A2) → `ex:jac` curve
Jacobian. §5 is "formal, without the finiteness theorems", so A2/A3 shrink; but
`ex:jac` routes existence through `th:main` → the full Quot/Hilbert engine,
which is **irreducible**. Net ~5100 LOC (A1 ~3775 fixed; A2 ~800; A3 ~500 via
curve `Pic⁰=Pic^τ`). Picked over Route B (Symⁿ+Stein). **Goal-alignment gap:**
the pointed positive-genus Albanese universal property (`isAlbaneseFor` for
`g ≥ 1` — every `f : C → A` killing `P` factors uniquely through `Pic⁰`) is
goal-required and NOT yet itemised in the ~5100; the true budget is higher.

37	> `df ∈ H^0(C, Hom(f^*Ω_A, Ω_C))`; with `f^*Ω_A ≅ O_C^{⊕g}` (gap i = the EXCISED
38	> globalisation) this is `H^0(C, Ω_C^{⊕g}) = (H^0(C,Ω_C))^{⊕g}`, so
39	> **`df = 0 ⟺ H^0(C,Ω_C) = 0`** (gap ii = Serre duality on a curve).
40	
41	On an affine chart `V`, `Ω_C(V)` is *free of rank 1* (nonzero), so the
42	chart-by-chart KDM stack the project already built **cannot detect `df=0`** — the
43	vanishing only materialises on global sections. This is precisely why the iter-144
44	chart-algebra pivot could build the converse but not `df=0`. **No minimal in-tree
45	assembly exists**; the directive's hoped-for "lighter route" is not present in
46	`b80f227`.
47	
48	## Answers to the ranked questions
49	
50	1. **Group-scheme/group-object cotangent triviality — Q1**: the ONLY Mathlib idiom
51	   for "(co)tangent of a group is trivial / translation-invariant" is the
52	   smooth-manifold stack `Mathlib.Geometry.Manifold.GroupLieAlgebra`
53	   (`addInvariantVectorField` builds a global frame from one identity vector;
54	   `mpullback_addInvariantVectorField` is its translation-invariance) and
55	   `Mathlib.Geometry.Manifold.Algebra.LeftInvariantDerivation`
56	   (`left_invariant`, `instLieAlgebra`). It is structurally identical to the
57	   excised `shearMulRight`/`mulRight_globalises_cotangent`, but built on
58	   `ChartedSpace`/`TangentSpace`/`VectorField.mpullback`/`LieAddGroup` with no
59	   scheme-side API — porting = rebuilding the excised presheaf globalisation.
60	   In **algebraic geometry**: ABSENT (`loogle "GrpObj, KaehlerDifferential"` → no
61	   results), confirming `cotangent-vanishing-pile.md` (iter-126).
62	   → **Mathlib has nothing portable; reuse only as a design template.**
63	
64	2. **`H^0(C, Ω_C)=0` for genus 0 — Q2**: ABSENT, and this is the true bottleneck.
65	   The link `dim H^0(Ω) = dim H^1(O)` *is* Serre duality. No Serre duality, no
66	   dualizing/canonical sheaf, no Riemann–Roch in `b80f227` (reaffirms
67	   `serre-duality.md` iter-110, ~3000–8000 LOC). The project's `Cohomology/*`
68	   stack computes `H^1(O_C)` but (verified) has **no H⁰-vanishing lemma, no
69	   direct-sum lemma, and no genus↔Ω bridge**. The genus-0 hypothesis
70	   (`H^1(O_C)=0`, per `Genus.lean`) does NOT give `H^0(Ω_C)=0` without the
71	   duality. → **Mathlib has nothing — must route through the (deferred) Serre
72	   duality, or via a `C ≅ ℙ¹` chart identification that `p1-hedge-...md` ruled
73	   NOT-VIABLE.**
74	
75	3. **"Map out of trivial bundle into no-sections sheaf is zero" — Q3**: PRESENT and
76	   cheap at the **module level**: `Submodule.linearMap_eq_zero_iff_of_span_eq_top`
77	   (`f=0 ↔ f` kills a spanning set), `LinearMap.ker_eq_top`,
78	   `AlternatingMap.map_basis_eq_zero_iff`; at the sheaf-Hom level
79	   `Scheme.Modules.Hom.zero_app`. This is genuinely portable (~5–20 LOC) but is
80	   **only the final glue** — it presupposes gaps (i) and (ii) already closed and
81	   contributes nothing to the hard content.
82	
83	4. **Sidestep check — Q4**: every candidate is ABSENT in `b80f227`:
84	   - scheme-level rigidity of morphisms to group schemes / abelian varieties —
85	     ABSENT;
86	   - "rational curve on an abelian variety is constant" — ABSENT;
87	   - Mumford Rigidity Lemma — ABSENT;
88	   - "morphism from proper geom-connected to affine/separated is constant" — not
89	     packaged (only `Scheme.RationalMap`, `FormallyUnramified`, affine-morphism
90	     plumbing surface). Moreover the classical proof of "genus-0 → AV constant"
91	     *itself* routes through `H^0(Ω_C)=0` (or Albanese, also absent), so **no
92	     sidestep eliminates the Q2 dependence.** Confirmed: no phantoms to chase.

97	of `A`} + {Serre-duality `H^0(C,Ω_C)=0`}, both already-deferred multi-thousand-LOC

106	identity + translation-invariance), not as an importable dependency.
107

109	- **Mumford Rigidity Lemma as a differential-free sidestep**: not in Mathlib, and
110	  it is a different theorem (commutativity / hom-up-to-translation of AVs), not the
111	  tool for "rational curve is constant"; that still needs `H^0(Ω_C)=0` or Albanese.

input.old_string:
**Soundness rules (operational, project-wide):**

- **No new axioms.** Every closed declaration `lean_verify`s to
  kernel-only axioms (`propext, Classical.choice, Quot.sound`).
- **Sorry-must-be-named-declaration.** A `sorry` must be the body of
  a top-level named declaration. It must NOT be embedded inside a
  `letI` / `have` / anonymous-`fun` body inside a different
  declaration. Without this discipline, a buried `sorry` propagates
  a sorry-tainted value through `simp` consumers without surfacing a
  `declaration uses sorry` warning at the wrapping declaration.

inside another decl (a buried `sorry` propagates a tainted value through `simp`
consumers without surfacing a `declaration uses sorry` warning).

# Blueprint Writer Directive

## Slug
rigidity-regate

## Target chapter
blueprint/src/chapters/RigidityKbar.tex

## Strategy context

The chart-algebra ring/chart envelope is CLOSED and axiom-clean:
`KaehlerDifferential.mem_range_algebraMap_of_D_eq_zero` (KDM),
`constants_integral_over_base_field`, and the delegate
`df_zero_factors_through_constant_on_chart`. These supply the **downstream half**
of the genus-0 rigidity bridge: "if `df = 0` then `f : C → A` factors through
`Spec k̄` (is constant)".

**A decisive `mathlib-analogist` consult (`analogies/df-zero-production-iter155.md`)
established a strategy-level finding that this chapter currently contradicts:**

> Producing the keystone input `df = 0` (vanishing of `df : f^*Ω_{A/k̄} → Ω_{C/k̄}`)
> is **irreducibly a global-sections fact**. `df ∈ H^0(C, Hom(f^*Ω_A, Ω_C))`; given
> the global trivialisation `f^*Ω_A ≅ O_C^{⊕g}`, this is `(H^0(C, Ω_C))^{⊕g}`, so
> **`df = 0 ⟺ H^0(C, Ω_C) = 0`**. The chart-by-chart KDM stack **CANNOT** detect
> `df = 0`: on each affine chart `V`, `Ω_C(V)` is *free of rank 1* (nonzero). The
> vanishing materialises only on global sections.

Therefore the chapter's recurring claim that the chart-algebra route **avoids
Serre duality** (e.g. §"shared cotangent-vanishing pile" piece (iv) framed as
"DEFERRED, not on the C.2.d critical path"; the "Iter-144 chart-algebra envelope
for piece (ii)" §"No-Serre-duality layer" claiming the chart-Čech β-chain
"replaces that route") is **FALSE**. `df = 0` requires BOTH:
- **(gap i) global `Ω_{A/k̄}` cotangent-triviality** (`Ω_A ≅ O_A^{⊕g}`, generated by
  translation-invariant differentials). The presheaf globalisation that would
  build this (`mulRight_globalises_cotangent`) was **EXCISED from the Lean tree
  (iter-145)**; `GrpObj.cotangentSpaceAtIdentity` + `shearMulRight` survive only as
  a *design template*. ~800–1500 LOC. Mathlib's only "group cotangent is trivial"
  idiom is the smooth-manifold `GroupLieAlgebra`, NOT portable to schemes.
- **(gap ii) Serre duality `H^0(C, Ω_{C/k̄}) = 0`** for the genus-0 curve. Mathlib
  `b80f227` has no dualizing sheaf / Riemann–Roch; the project's `Cohomology/*`
  stack computes `H^1(O_C)` but has **no `H^0`-vanishing lemma, no genus↔Ω bridge**.
  `H^1(O_C) = 0` (genus 0) does NOT yield `H^0(Ω_C) = 0` without the duality.
  ~3000–8000 LOC.

**Disposition (decided):** `rigidity_over_kbar` remains a NAMED GAP `sorry`; the
project will not open a "produce df=0" prover lane until one of two candidate
closure routes is built. Decision between them is deferred (leading open strategic
question), so the chapter must present BOTH as gated options, committing to
neither:
- **Route (a):** build gaps (i) + (ii) directly.
- **Route (b):** the dual-abelian-variety keystone — "morphisms `ℙ¹_{k̄} → A` are
  constant because `Pic⁰(ℙ¹) = 0`". Earlier rejected as "needs `Pic⁰`", but since
  the positive-genus arm needs Route A's `Pic⁰` engine REGARDLESS, route (b) may
  amortise the shared machinery and be globally cheaper than a standalone Serre
  duality build.

## Required changes

1. **Correct the false "chart-algebra avoids Serre duality" framing** wherever it
   appears (the piece-(iv) "DEFERRED, not on C.2.d critical path" disposition; the
   "No-Serre-duality layer" paragraph; any prose asserting the chart-Čech β-chain
   replaces the `H^0(C,Ω)=0` route). Replace with the analogist's global-sections
   decomposition: `df = 0 ⟺ H^0(C,Ω_C)=0` (given trivialisation), so Serre duality
   `H^0(C,Ω_C)=0` is now **on the critical path**, not avoided.

2. **Re-scope `thm:rigidity_over_kbar` (the proof decomposition C.2.b–C.2.e) as a
   gated NAMED GAP.** Model the disclosure on how `Jacobian.tex` discloses its
   deferred `thm:nonempty_jacobianWitness` foundational gap (honest, thorough,
   explicitly flagged — the iter-155 blueprint-reviewer praised that style). State
   plainly: the substantive case (C.2.d, ruling out a 1-dimensional image) reduces
   to `df = 0`, whose production is gated on gaps (i)+(ii) [route a] or on the
   dual-AV keystone [route b]; the chart-algebra envelope provides only the
   converse "df=0 ⟹ constant". Present both routes; commit to neither.

3. **Fix the `lem:Scheme_Over_ext_of_diff_zero` block:**
   - Its Step-1 prose cites `lem:GrpObj_mulRight_globalises`
     (`mulRight_globalises_cotangent`), which was **EXCISED iter-145**. Remove the
     load-bearing dependency; note it is a design template only, not a present decl.
   - Reconcile the **statement-vs-Lean signature divergence**: the current Lean
     `Scheme.Over.ext_of_diff_zero` is a THIN wrapper of `ext_of_eqOnOpen`
     (signature: `[IsSeparated A.hom] [IsReduced C.left] [GeometricallyIrreducible
     C.hom]`, consumes `eqOnOpen U` directly — NO `df = dg`, no genus-0, no
     group-scheme hyps). The blueprint *statement* asserts the substantive
     `df = dg` + genus-0 + group-scheme form. Make the chapter say clearly: the
     present Lean decl is the thin wrapper; the substantive `df = dg` form (Steps
     1–3) is the **gated refinement**, inert until `df = 0` is producible (gaps
     i+ii / route b). Keep Steps 1–3 as the eventual closing-glue recipe but mark
     them gated.
   - Note the cheap final glue available once `df=0` holds:
     `Submodule.linearMap_eq_zero_iff_of_span_eq_top` [verified] (a map out of a
     trivial/spanned source killing the spanning set is zero).

4. **C.2.c image-dimension dichotomy:** flag that it names no present Mathlib infra
   (image-of-proper-map closed/irreducible, scheme dimension); it is part of the
   gated assembly, to be detailed when a route is chosen.

5. **Prune the stale statement-block `\uses{}` at the KDM block** (around L2340):
   `lem:chart_algebra_isPushout_of_affine_product` +
   `lem:KaehlerDifferential_constants_in_chart_of_proper_curve` — the live (FT)
   route consumes neither (the proof-block `\uses` was already pruned). Demote the
   now-orphaned `lem:KaehlerDifferential_constants_in_chart_of_proper_curve` to an
   off-path record (mirror the descope language used elsewhere). Do NOT touch the
   KDM / `constants_integral` / `df_zero_factors` PROOF blocks otherwise — they are
   closed and axiom-clean.

6. **Add a `% archon:covers` line** near the top of the chapter so the HARD-GATE
   file→chapter mapping is explicit:
   `% archon:covers RigidityKbar.lean Cotangent/ChartAlgebra.lean`
   (the blueprint content for both files lives in this chapter; `GrpObj.lean` keeps
   its own chapter).

## References

- `analogies/df-zero-production-iter155.md` (the analogist's global-sections
  decomposition + analogue table) — the authoritative source for the re-scope.
- `analogies/serre-duality.md`, `analogies/p1-hedge-iter138.md`,
  `analogies/cotangent-vanishing-pile.md` — prior consults consistent with this
  finding (LOC estimates, the `C ≅ ℙ¹` route ruled not-viable).
- The Mumford `% SOURCE:` on `thm:rigidity_over_kbar` is honestly disclosed as
  paywalled / not bundled — do NOT fabricate a `% SOURCE QUOTE:`; leave the honest
  disclosure. The global-sections df=0 decomposition is Archon-original analysis
  (no external source line needed).

## Out of scope

- Do NOT modify the proof blocks of KDM (`lem:KaehlerDifferential_mem_range_algebraMap_of_D_eq_zero`),
  `lem:constants_integral_over_base_field`, or
  `lem:chart_algebra_df_zero_factors_through_constant_on_chart` — closed, axiom-clean.
- Do NOT add or remove `\leanok` or `\mathlibok` markers (managed by sync_leanok /
  review).
- Do NOT edit any other chapter, any `.lean` file, or `content.tex`. Cross-chapter
  issues (e.g. `Jacobian.tex` C.2.d's parallel df=0 framing) go in your report's
  "Notes for Plan Agent", not fixed here.
- Do NOT commit to route (a) vs (b); present both as gated.
